articles.expand: In the likes of Germany, France, Switzerland, and Austria, there will be a public holiday in observance of Whit Monday (general naming convention). However, the Euronext and Xetra bourses/platforms will be open for trading although the Swiss stock exchange will be closed. articles.title: AUD/JPY Price Forecast: Gains momentum, holding bullish bias above 100-day EMA
articles.expand: The AUD/JPY cross gains ground to around 113.80 during the early European trading hours on Monday. Hopes of a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz buoyed risk appetite, supporting the Australian Dollar (AUD) against the Japanese Yen (JPY).

articles.title: White House Adviser Hassett says ending Iran war may create room for Fed rate cut
articles.expand: US President Donald Trump’s chief economic adviser at the White House, Kevin Hassett, said on Monday that he is confident that an eventual drop in oil prices will create space for the US Federal Reserve (Fed) to cut the interest rates.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD jumps to near 1.3480 amid hopes of the US-Iran deal
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair is up 0.35% to near 1.3480 during the Asian trading session on Monday. The Cable trades firmly as market sentiment for riskier assets has improved significantly due to increased hopes of a deal between the United States (US) and Iran.

articles.title: Japanese Yen gains traction on US-Iran peace progress
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair edges lower to around 158.85, snapping the two-day winning streak during the Asian trading hours on Monday. The US Dollar (USD) weakens against the Japanese Yen (JPY) amid signs of a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Swiss Franc advances as risk-on mood weakens USD

articles.title: US Dollar Index weakens to near 99.00 on US-Iran peace deal hopes
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), an index of the value of the US Dollar (USD) measured against a basket of six world currencies, currently trades near 99.05 during the Asian trading hours on Monday. The DXY loses momentum as the prospect of a deal to end the Iran war buoyed risk appetite.

articles.title: Lagarde speech: ECB set to upgrade its inflation forecast in June
articles.expand: In an Italian talkshow Che Tempo Che Fa on Sunday, the European Central Bank (ECB) President Christine Lagarde said that the central bank is likely to raise the inflation forecast at its monetary policy meeting next month.

articles.title: Gold climbs above $4,550 on US‑Iran deal hopes
articles.expand: Gold price (XAU/USD) rises to near $4,570 during the early Asian trading hours on Monday. The precious metal attracts some buyers on weaker US Dollar (USD) after the reports that the United States (US) and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: WTI tumbles 5% on renewed hopes of the Strait of Hormuz reopening
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) – the US oil benchmark – has witnessed a steep bearish opening gap on Monday, now losing nearly 5%, on its way to surrendering the $90 threshold.

articles.title: Britain's Youth Employment Crisis and the Rising Cost of Hiring

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Awaiting more war-related headlines and central banks

War-related headlines kept driving financial markets, coupled with mounting speculation that the United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) will deliver a rate hike before the year is over.
